methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Corvex Systems is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Jason Blick that has raised $5M+. The sector peer universe comprises 1 public company and 8 private firms, including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

Only one valuation method was applicable to this analysis. The stage-progression approach, which uses the $5M total raised as a proxy for baseline value and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage progression, yields an implied valuation of $12M. Peer-median triangulation was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics and manufacturing space—while 11 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three qualified under narrow filters for stage and valuation tier, making sector-wide medians unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median of $12M. This range reflects limited methodological diversity; the estimate rests substantially on stage-typical progression multiples rather than on direct peer benchmarking. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer valuation data and the assumption that the $5M raised represents fair baseline value. Additional clarity on Corvex's technology differentiation, customer traction, and burn rate would strengthen conviction in this range and inform whether upside or downside adjustments are warranted relative to the $12M median.
